title: 「whose」句型
titleEn: Using whose to express ownership or a close connection

omissions: The PDF title has no separate English subtitle; titleEn is an editorial translation.

formula: Person / animal / place / organisation / thing + whose + noun + verb + other information

formulas.formula: Person / animal / place / organisation / thing + whose + noun + verb + other information

example: I went to a restaurant whose decoration was superb.
exampleZh: 我去過一間餐廳，它的裝潢非常出色。

examples.en: I went to a restaurant whose decoration was superb.
examples.zh: 我去過一間餐廳，它的裝潢非常出色。
examples.highlight: I went to a restaurant whose decoration was superb.

examples.en: I met a girl. Her brother is in my class.
examples.zh: 我遇到一個女孩。她的哥哥在我班上。
examples.highlight: I met a girl. Her brother is in my class.

examples.en: I met a girl whose brother is in my class.
examples.zh: 我遇到一個女孩，她的哥哥在我班上。
examples.highlight: I met a girl whose brother is in my class.

zh: whose 後面的名詞屬於或與 whose 前面的人或事物有密切關係。

rules.en: Use whose + noun. Do not add his, her, its or their after whose.
rules.zh: whose 後面直接加名詞，不可以再加入 his、her、its 或 their。
examples: Correct: a girl whose brother is in my class
Incorrect: a girl whose her brother is in my class

rules.en: The verb agrees with the noun after whose.
rules.zh: 動詞的單複數由 whose 後面的名詞決定。

rules.en: Whose can describe people and things.
rules.zh: whose 不但可以描述人，也可以描述動物、地方、機構和物件。
examples: a student whose answer was correct
a hotel whose rooms were clean

rules.en: Do not confuse whose with who’s. Whose means of whom / of which; who’s means who is or who has.
rules.zh: 不要混淆 whose 與 who’s。whose 表示「誰的／它的」，沒有撇號；who’s 表示 who is 或 who has，有撇號。

rules.en: Use commas for additional information about a known person or thing.
rules.zh: 當 whose 子句只是補充一個已知人物或事物的額外資料時，子句前後通常需要使用逗號。
examples: Chloe, whose father teaches music, can play three instruments.

benefits.en: It clearly shows ownership or connection. This structure makes it clear which person or thing a detail is connected with.
benefits.zh: 這個句型能清楚顯示某件事物屬於誰，或某項資料與哪一個人或事物有關。

benefits.en: It joins two related sentences. Instead of writing two separate sentences, you can combine the information into one complete sentence.
benefits.zh: 你可以把兩個有關連的句子合併成一句，不需要把資料分開表達。

benefits.en: It avoids repeating the same noun. You do not need to repeat words such as the girl, the student, the company or the building.
benefits.zh: 它能避免重複使用「那個女孩」、「那名學生」、「那間公司」或「那幢建築物」等名詞。

benefits.en: It helps identify the exact person or thing. You can add an important detail to show exactly which person, place or object you mean.
benefits.zh: 你可以加入一項重要資料，讓讀者清楚知道你所指的是哪一個人、地方或物件。

benefits.en: It makes descriptions smoother and more detailed. This structure is useful when describing people, families, animals, places, organisations and objects in conversations, stories, essays and reports.
benefits.zh: 這個句型適合在對話、故事、作文和報告中描述人物、家庭、動物、地方、機構和物件，令內容更流暢和具體。

sourceOmissions: The PDF title has no separate English subtitle; titleEn is an editorial translation.

en: Rewrite each sentence or pair of sentences using whose to join the related ideas.
The first word of each answer has been provided.
zh: 使用 whose 合併相關意思，並改寫以下句子。
每題已提供答案的第一個字。
